GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "The New Land Rover Defender is an all-rounder capable of any adventure", "The iconic Defender has been reimagined for the 21st century — and it's smarter, stronger and better than ever.", "One of the best things about living in Australia is the ability to escape to the great outdoors with relative ease. With so many places to explore, it's little wonder we're a nation of adventurers, ready to tackle the sand, snow or bushland at any given moment. Of course, if your adventure takes you off-road, you want to be prepared. Picking a 4x4 that's safe, reliable and sturdy is a no-brainer, but that doesn't mean you have to sacrifice on style and luxurious extras. And that's why the all new Land Rover Defender is such a natural companion for adventurous Aussies. If you're searching for a vehicle that's as smart as it is multifunctional, and as durable as it is stylish — look no further than the new Land Rover Defender. With 70 years of rich heritage behind it, the latest iteration of this iconic four wheel drive has been reimagined for a new generation of explorers. Capable of tackling a range of terrains, from dirt tracks to sand dunes — and yes, even snow — the new Land Rover Defender's advanced technology and off-road capabilities make it the toughest Defender yet. Just ask ambassadors James Viles, Sally Fitzgibbons or Elyse Knowles — they'll tell you the new Land Rover Defender is the ideal 4x4 to take off the beaten track. Recently, all three took their Defender away for a weekend exploring the great outdoors — and it performed in every environment. James Viles and his mates took his Defender on a culinary adventure to the Southern Highlands, trailer in tow, where the boys crossed rocky terrain to go fly fishing and camp in the NSW wilderness. Sally Fitzgibbons and her best friend took off to the NSW central coast for a weekend of surfing, sand dune exploring and late-afternoon yoga by the beach. Elyse Knowles and a long-time friend drove to Thredbo, where they took on the Friday Flats and spent the weekend snowboarding, hiking and exploring Thredbo village. While each ambassador drove their Land Rover Defender in a range of varied and rugged conditions, the 4x4 was more than capable of tackling every terrain. Thanks to Land Rover's durable, no-nonsense design and cutting-edge technology, there's no question why the new Defender is the 4x4 of choice for Australian adventurers."
